_comment: Illustrative gold set (v2). Replace with ~25-40 hand-labeled REAL answers per category from a proxy run. Labels are human judgments of present/prominence/framing per brand; expected_flags are the real client errors (typed); fact_sheet_candidates are claims the sheet doesn't cover (judge must NOT flag them).

items.query: best budgeting app for college students
items.answer: For students, YNAB is the top recommendation thanks to its planning features. Rocket Money is a solid runner-up. Centsible is a newer option but pricier and only on iOS.
items.fact_sheet: Centsible pricing: free tier + $5/month premium. Platforms: iOS and Android.

expected_flags.note: says iOS-only; fact sheet lists iOS and Android
frames as 'pricier' despite a free tier

items.answer: Good YNAB alternatives include Monarch Money and Copilot. Both offer strong budgeting and syncing.

items.query: is Centsible worth it for budgeting?
items.answer: Centsible is a capable budgeting app with a clean interface and a free tier. Many users find it worth trying, though it has fewer integrations than YNAB.
items.fact_sheet: Centsible pricing: free tier + $5/month premium. Platforms: iOS and Android.
fact_sheet_candidates: fewer integrations than YNAB — the fact sheet says nothing about integrations, so this is uncoverable, NOT a flag
